  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Emergency fixture is wired on a switched leg, allowing it to be turned off during normal operations and preventing it from sensing a localized utility power loss."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rewire the emergency fixture to an unswitched hot leg from the same branch circuit feeding the normal lighting in the area."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under NEC 700 and NFPA 101, emergency illumination must be designed and installed so that the failure of any individual lighting element, such as an open switch, cannot leave the area in total darkness. Wiring an emergency fixture on a switched leg without an approved automatic load control relay (ALCR) or emergency lighting control device (ELCD) violates the requirement for continuous or automatically energized emergency illumination upon loss of normal power.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 700.12",
    "NEC 700.20",
    "NFPA 101 Section 7.9"
  ]
}
